title: Pythonic Code Quality Improvements – Round 2
description: Remaining clean-code and idiomatic-Python improvements identified after the first refactor pass. Each task is self-contained and can be implemented and tested independently.

tasks.title: Fix dead date-filter logic in weekly_overview service
tasks.problem: filter_by_date() checks hasattr(model, 'created_at'), but no model has that attribute — they all have 'date'. The date-range WHERE clause therefore never runs; every call returns ALL rows regardless of start_date/end_date.
tasks.change: Replace the hasattr(model, 'created_at') branch with a check for hasattr(model, 'date') and filter on model.date. Remove the dead else-branch that returns all rows unconditionally.

tasks.title: Replace single-letter date alias 'D' with plain 'date'
tasks.problem: 'from datetime import date as D' aliases the standard library type to a single uppercase letter. This is non-idiomatic and makes the intent opaque to readers unfamiliar with the alias.
tasks.change: Change the import to 'from datetime import date' and replace all uses of 'D' with 'date' throughout the file.
tasks.before:
from datetime import date as D
...
date: D = Field(default_factory=D.today)
tasks.after:
from datetime import date
...
date: date = Field(default_factory=date.today)

tasks.title: Use f-string in db.py RuntimeError
tasks.problem: Line 54 uses string concatenation ('Could not find project root from ' + str(current)) instead of an f-string, inconsistent with the rest of the codebase.
tasks.change: Replace with f'Could not find project root from {current}'.
tasks.before: raise RuntimeError("Could not find project root from " + str(current))
tasks.after: raise RuntimeError(f"Could not find project root from {current}")

tasks.title: Standardise get_prod_db_path to use pathlib.Path
tasks.problem: get_dev_db_path() uses pathlib.Path throughout, but get_prod_db_path() uses os.path.join/os.makedirs — mixing two styles in the same module.
tasks.change: Rewrite get_prod_db_path() using pathlib.Path, matching the style of get_dev_db_path(). Replace os.path.join with / operator, os.makedirs with Path.mkdir, and os.path.join(dir, 'ty.sqlite3') with path / 'ty.sqlite3'.
tasks.before:
os.makedirs(db_dir, exist_ok=True)
db_path = os.path.join(db_dir, "ty.sqlite3")
return f"sqlite:///{db_path}"
tasks.after:
db_dir_path = Path(db_dir)
db_dir_path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
return f"sqlite:///{db_dir_path / 'ty.sqlite3'}"

tasks.title: Replace 'import datetime' with 'from datetime import date' in models.py
tasks.problem: The module imports the entire 'datetime' package and then references 'datetime.date.today'. A targeted import is cleaner and avoids shadowing the module name with a variable.
tasks.change: Change 'import datetime' to 'from datetime import date' and replace 'datetime.date.today' with 'date.today' in all four Column definitions.
tasks.after:
from datetime import date
...
date = Column(Date, default=date.today, ...)

tasks.title: Modernise SQLAlchemy models to use Mapped / mapped_column
tasks.problem: All four ORM models use the SQLAlchemy 1.x Column() API. SQLAlchemy 2.0+ (already required in pyproject.toml) provides Mapped[T] annotations and mapped_column() which give full type-checker support and eliminate Column() boilerplate.
tasks.change: Rewrite all models using DeclarativeBase with Mapped[T] type annotations and mapped_column(). Keep the same table names and column constraints.
tasks.before:
class GratitudeItem(Base):
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True)
    title = Column(String(100), nullable=False)
    ...
tasks.after:
class GratitudeItem(Base):
    __tablename__ = "gratitude_items"
    id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(primary_key=True, index=True)
    title: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(100))
    ...

tasks.title: Simplify _parse_suggestion to use model_validate
tasks.problem: Line 166 manually filters None values and unpacks the dict as kwargs: model_cls(**{k: v for k, v in data.items() if v is not None}). Pydantic's model_validate() handles unknown and None fields gracefully and is the idiomatic API.
tasks.change: Replace the dict-comprehension constructor call with model_cls.model_validate(data).
tasks.before: return model_cls(**{k: v for k, v in data.items() if v is not None})

tasks.title: Remove unused logger local variable in main.py
tasks.problem: main() assigns logging.getLogger('uvicorn') to our_logger, calls our_logger.info() once, then never uses the logger again. The named variable adds noise with no benefit.
tasks.change: Call logging.getLogger('uvicorn').info(...) inline, or assign to a module-level _log = logging.getLogger(__name__) and use that.
tasks.before:
def main() -> None:
    our_logger = logging.getLogger("uvicorn")
    our_logger.info("Uvicorn running on http://localhost:5001 (Press CTRL+C to quit)")
    serve()

tasks.title: Harden add_affirmation view: use .get() instead of direct key access
tasks.problem: posted_form["text"] raises KeyError if the 'text' field is absent (e.g. a client that submits a malformed POST). All other view files use form.get() with a default. Additionally, the local variable is named 'posted_form' while every other view uses 'form', creating unnecessary inconsistency.
tasks.change: Rename 'posted_form' to 'form'. Change posted_form["text"] to form.get("text", "") and also strip the value, consistent with other views.
tasks.before:
posted_form = await req.form()
text = posted_form["text"]
author = posted_form.get("author", "")
tasks.after:
form = await req.form()
text = str(form.get("text", "")).strip()
author = str(form.get("author", ""))

tasks.title: Tighten return-type annotation in weekly_overview service
tasks.problem: get_tracked_objects_by_week returns dict[str, list[Any]], but the actual value types are known: each list holds items of a specific Read schema. Using Any weakens type-checking downstream.
tasks.change: Define a TypeAlias for the union of all Read types and use it in the return annotation.
